mapping.a_element: a neuron's membrane potential crossing a firing threshold to emit a spike
mapping.b_element: an agent's aggregate exposure crossing its activation threshold to turn to harmful behavior

mapping.a_element: the fixed spike/firing threshold of a single neuron

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the local rule that accumulated input crossing a threshold triggers a discrete event. Beyond that the machinery diverges: paper A is a survey of single-entity, continuous/discrete dynamical models of one neuron's membrane potential (reversible, self-resetting spiking, no network), whereas paper B is a network-level irreversible threshold-cascade whose transferable content is a collective phase transition — a necessary-and-sufficient structural stability condition, signed-influence convex repair, and a mean-field homophily threshold — none of which has a counterpart in a single-neuron survey.
